title: Enhanced Home Personal Care (HPC+)
text:
Enhanced Home Personal Care (HPC+) - SupportGoWhere
Enhanced Home Personal Care (HPC+)
Share link
Support frail or homebound seniors who require assistance with activities of daily living, and/or general support with routine tasks in the community.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Fees payable
Referral required
Collapse all sections
About this support

Provided by trained care professionals to assist seniors and their caregivers with:

Personal care needs

Activities of Daily Living (e.g. showering, feeding)

Mind stimulating activities

Simple maintenance exercises prescribed by physiotherapist, occupational therapist, or speech therapist

Elder-sitting and respite for caregivers

General support with routine tasks (e.g. regular check-ins, medication reminders, help with errands)

Monitoring and responding to falls 24/7 using digital devices the client has installed at home

Household chores (e.g. sweeping floors, cleaning fans and windows, and washing toilets)

Who is this for?

Senior who:

Requires personal services to continue living well at home

Is a Singapore Citizen (SC), Permanent Resident (PR) or foreigner

Caregiver who:

Needs support in their caregiving duties

Is a Singapore Citizen (SC), Permanent Resident (PR) or foreigner

What to expect?

Subsidies are available depending on the means-test result.

Only applicable to SC and PR

Applicants for care services need to fulfil the services’ eligibility criteria and providers’ service boundaries, if any, and enrolments are subject to availability of vacancies.

How to apply?

You will need to get a referral from a hospital, polyclinic or GP who is familiar with your loved ones’ condition and needs.

You may also contact the service provider for a discussion. Visit the Agency for Integrated Care (AIC)’s website to search for service providers by postal code, address or your device’s current location.

For further assistance, contact the AIC at 1800 650 6060, email enquiries@aic.sg or walk into a nearby AIC Link.

Information last updated on 19 May 2026
